description: This bar chart presents the Vickers microhardness (in HV) of as-cast Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–xBe low-density steels as a function of beryllium content (x = 0, 0.5, 1.0, and 1.5). Red bars denote austenite hardness and blue bars denote ferrite hardness. In the 0Be and 0.5Be steels, only austenite is detected, exhibiting hardness values of 453.31 HV and 462.88 HV, respectively, with no ferrite phase present. In contrast, the 1.0Be and 1.5Be steels are dual-phase structures containing both austenite and ferrite; the austenite measures 475.80 HV and 479.74 HV, while the ferrite measures 437.40 HV and 452.54 HV, respectively. The data reveal a consistent upward trend in austenite hardness with increasing Be addition. The ferrite hardness similarly increases with Be content in the dual-phase alloys. The caption and text indicate that the matrix hardening upon Be addition is partly attributed to the formation of Be-containing compounds, and note that metallographic hardness of the Be–Fe phase in 0.5Be steel could not be obtained due to instrumental limitations. Overall, the figure demonstrates that Be alloying effectively enhances the hardness of both austenite and ferrite in these cast steels.
